title: El Viaje Misterioso De Nuestro Jomer The

jokes.text: D'oh! [Screams]
jokes.setup: Opening credits crash sequence
jokes.punchline: Homer's signature frustrated exclamation as he crashes
jokes.explanation: Iconic catchphrase, perfect character integration but routine execution

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: D'oh!

jokes.text: Oh, why do they have to put all this crud in my newspaper?
jokes.setup: Homer reading newspaper
jokes.punchline: Homer dismisses news and arts as 'crud'
jokes.explanation: Good character work showing Homer's anti-intellectual attitude

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: News and arts? That's all crud.

jokes.text: 'World.' 'The Arts.' 'Religion.' Aha. Here it is. 'Kickin' Back!'
jokes.setup: Homer dismisses important newspaper sections
jokes.punchline: He only wants the trivial entertainment section
jokes.explanation: Sharp satire of anti-intellectualism, good escalating structure

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Sports? Comics? I don't have time for that garbage. Just give me the section with the celebrity gossip and TV listings.

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: Homer, those are the only sections you ever read.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Exactly! Finally, someone who understands me.

jokes.text: Not me. I'm more of a mail tamperer.
jokes.setup: Family denying they cut out newspaper article
jokes.punchline: Bart casually admits to federal crime instead
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection and character work - Bart's casual criminality is perfectly in character

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: Oh, I didn't cut it out. I photocopied it.

jokes.text: gussy up these curtains. [Groans]
jokes.setup: Lisa holding scissors, trying to explain why
jokes.punchline: Weak excuse followed by her own groan of defeat
jokes.explanation: Lisa's self-awareness about her bad lie creates the humor

dialogue.speaker: Lisa
dialogue.line: I was just trying to... to trim the hedges.

jokes.text: Yeah. It's a lazy, dog-dangling afternoon.
jokes.setup: Homer trying to sound casual about his afternoon
jokes.punchline: Bizarre phrase 'dog-dangling' makes no sense
jokes.explanation: Homer's malapropism creates memorable absurdist humor

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Well, I've just been... dog-dangling all afternoon.

jokes.text: Well, I just felt like filling the house with the rich, satisfying smell of tobacco.
jokes.setup: Homer notices Marge smoking, which she never does
jokes.punchline: Marge's obviously fake excuse delivered in advertising language
jokes.explanation: Good satire of tobacco advertising mixed with Marge's terrible lying

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Marge, are you smoking?

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: Oh Homer, I'm not smoking. I'm just enjoying the fresh, rich taste of Cigarettes™ — now available in Menthol Fresh and Smooth Vanilla. Cigarettes™: because sometimes a woman needs a moment to herself.

jokes.text: [Whistles] You're nuts.
jokes.setup: Marge gives ridiculous excuse for smoking
jokes.punchline: Homer's casual dismissal with a whistle
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective reaction beat

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: I only smoke when I'm stressed, angry, or sad.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Mmm, those are the only times worth being alive.

jokes.text: Every time you go to that cook-off, you get drunk as a poet on payday.
jokes.setup: Marge explaining why she hid the chili cook-off
jokes.punchline: Creative simile comparing Homer to a drunk poet
jokes.explanation: Brilliant original simile that's both unexpected and perfectly descriptive

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: I hid it because Homer gets competitive. He's like a drunk poet at a chili cook-off — he shows up, makes a mess, and nobody understands what he's trying to do.

jokes.text: Look at me. I'm a puffy pink cloud!
jokes.setup: Flashback to Homer's previous drunken behavior
jokes.punchline: Homer floating like a cloud at a children's event
jokes.explanation: Absurd visual combined with Homer's innocent joy creates perfect cringe comedy

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Wa-hoo! I'm a cloud! Float float float...

jokes.text: Oh, well, of course everything looks bad if you remember it.
jokes.setup: Marge shows Homer his embarrassing behavior
jokes.punchline: Homer dismisses memory as unreliable
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer logic - brilliantly absurd reasoning delivered with complete confidence

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: Homer, you were acting ridiculous at the party.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: I don't remember that. Memory is unreliable anyway.

jokes.text: Geez. Why don't you have a cigarette or somethin'?
jokes.setup: Marge nagging Homer about drinking
jokes.punchline: Homer sarcastically suggests her earlier smoking habit
jokes.explanation: Nice callback that shows Homer isn't completely oblivious

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: Homer, you drink too much. You need to cut back on the beer.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Oh yeah? Well maybe you should cut back on the cigarettes like you did before.

jokes.text: I'm gonna go claim some valuables at the lost and found.
jokes.setup: Family splitting up at the chili cook-off
jokes.punchline: Bart plans to steal from lost and found
jokes.explanation: Bart's casual criminality perfectly in character, economically delivered

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: While you guys are busy stuffing your faces, I'm gonna hit the lost and found. Those tourists never come back for their stuff.

jokes.text: Eight spices. Oh, some must be doubles.
jokes.setup: Marge admiring a spice rack
jokes.punchline: Her excitement deflates as she realizes the obvious
jokes.explanation: Gentle humor about Marge's domestic interests and logical thinking

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: Oh, a spice rack! How wonderful!

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: Wait... we don't have any spices.

jokes.text: 'Oregano'? What the hell?
jokes.setup: Homer looking at spice rack
jokes.punchline: Homer confused by common spice
jokes.explanation: Homer's ignorance of basic cooking ingredients

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Paprika? Why would anyone need this when they have ketchup?

jokes.text: Less artsy, more fartsy!
jokes.setup: Marge lingering at handicrafts booth
jokes.punchline: Homer's crude rhyming demand for food
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer - crude but clever wordplay showing his priorities

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Marge, quit your crafts, I need some grub. Make me some food or I'll rub-a-dub-dub!

jokes.text: Fine. I'll come find you when I'm ready to stop having fun.
jokes.setup: Marge prefers handicrafts to food
jokes.punchline: Homer sarcastically defines fun as eating
jokes.explanation: Standard marital dynamic humor but well-executed for Homer

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Fun? That's what I call eating.

jokes.text: Stupor pooper.
jokes.setup: Homer walking away from Marge
jokes.punchline: Homer's childish insult playing on 'party pooper'
jokes.explanation: Childish but memorable pun that fits Homer's mentality

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Oh yeah? Well, you're a party pooper-scooper!

jokes.text: Oh. I think it's lovely that he said that- and that you believed him.
jokes.setup: Marge defending Homer's promise not to drink
jokes.punchline: Helen's passive-aggressive doubt about Homer's reliability
jokes.explanation: Perfect Helen Lovejoy - passive-aggressive gossip delivered with fake sweetness

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: Homer promised he wouldn't drink anymore.

dialogue.speaker: Helen Lovejoy
dialogue.line: Well, I'm sure he'll stick to it this time. I mean, what could possibly go wrong?

jokes.text: Now, Helen, let us not glory in Homer's binge drinking. There but for the grace of God goes Marge herself.
jokes.setup: Helen gossiping about Homer
jokes.punchline: Reverend's fake piety while enabling the gossip
jokes.explanation: Good character work - Lovejoy's hypocritical righteousness while joining the gossip

dialogue.speaker: Helen Lovejoy
dialogue.line: Did you hear about Homer Simpson? He was seen at the bar last night!

dialogue.speaker: Reverend Lovejoy
dialogue.line: Oh my. Well, we mustn't judge. But please, do continue.

dialogue.speaker: Helen Lovejoy
dialogue.line: And he was with that Lenny fellow, drinking until closing time!

dialogue.speaker: Reverend Lovejoy
dialogue.line: How terribly un-Christian. Tell me everything.

jokes.text: Ooh, man. He's here. The dude with the fireproof stomach.
jokes.setup: Mysterious figure arrives at chili contest
jokes.punchline: Built-up reputation for eating spicy food
jokes.explanation: Sets up the legendary chili eater character effectively

dialogue.speaker: Man
dialogue.line: I'm here to enter the chili contest.

dialogue.speaker: Chief Wiggum
dialogue.line: Well, well, well, what's your name, friend?

dialogue.speaker: Man
dialogue.line: They call me the Chili King. I've eaten the hottest peppers in the world.

dialogue.speaker: Lard Lad
dialogue.line: The Chili King? I've heard stories about you!

dialogue.speaker: Man
dialogue.line: My reputation precedes me.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: So what's your secret?

dialogue.speaker: Man
dialogue.line: Years of building up a tolerance to spicy food. Started with jalapeños, moved to habaneros, then ghost peppers.

dialogue.speaker: Moe
dialogue.line: That's some serious dedication.

dialogue.speaker: Man
dialogue.line: When you've eaten as much fire as I have, regular chili is child's play.

jokes.text: They say he carved it himself from a bigger spoon.
jokes.setup: Reverent discussion of the chili champion
jokes.punchline: Ridiculous backstory about his spoon's origin
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd mythology around a simple eating utensil - unexpected and memorable

dialogue.speaker: Chief Wiggum
dialogue.line: And the secret ingredient is... love.

dialogue.speaker: Ned Flanders
dialogue.line: Well, I'm sure it's just delicious!

dialogue.speaker: Homer Simpson
dialogue.line: Actually, I found this spoon in 1947...

dialogue.speaker: Homer Simpson
dialogue.line: It belonged to a Portuguese pirate named Senor Spoon.

dialogue.speaker: Homer Simpson
dialogue.line: He sailed the seven seas looking for the world's greatest chili.

dialogue.speaker: Homer Simpson
dialogue.line: Legend has it he died with this spoon in his hand, still searching.

dialogue.speaker: Homer Simpson
dialogue.line: And now, I use it to make chili!

jokes.text: Oh, I admit it. It's only two alarm. Two and a half tops. I just wanted to be a big man in front of the kids.
jokes.setup: Vendor challenged on his 'five alarm' chili
jokes.punchline: Immediate confession of exaggeration and pathetic motivation
jokes.explanation: Good cringe comedy - the immediate collapse under pressure

dialogue.speaker: Chili Vendor
dialogue.line: Alright, alright, it's not really five alarms. I just wanted to impress people.

jokes.text: Daddy, are you going to jail? We'll see, Son. We'll see.
jokes.setup: Father caught lying about chili spiciness
jokes.punchline: Child's innocent question and father's ominous non-answer
jokes.explanation: Dark humor escalating the stakes absurdly - perfect comedic timing

dialogue.speaker: Ralph Wiggum
dialogue.line: Dad, what's wrong? Why are you sweating?

dialogue.speaker: Homer Simpson
dialogue.line: It's nothing, boy. Just... very hot chili.

dialogue.speaker: Ralph Wiggum
dialogue.line: But you said it wasn't spicy...

dialogue.speaker: Homer Simpson
dialogue.line: ...I know what I said.

jokes.text: Ma'am, I wouldn't honk the honk if I couldn't tonk the tonk.
jokes.setup: Woman worried about complicated square dancing
jokes.punchline: Country dancer's nonsense rhyming reassurance
jokes.explanation: Delightfully nonsensical country wisdom that sounds profound but means nothing

dialogue.speaker: Woman
dialogue.line: I'm worried this square dancing is too complicated!

dialogue.speaker: Country Dancer
dialogue.line: Don't you worry now, honey. Just do-si-do, and then you promenade, and the music will take you where you need to go. It's easy as pie, smooth as butter, neat as a cat's litter!

jokes.text: Hmm. A bland, timid entry... suitable perhaps for patients recovering from surgery.
jokes.setup: Chief Wiggum tasting someone's chili
jokes.punchline: Overly elaborate insult for mild chili
jokes.explanation: Good character work - Wiggum trying to sound sophisticated and failing

dialogue.speaker: Chief Wiggum
dialogue.line: Oh, this is just an insipid mush of pale, flavorless goop. It's like eating a cloud of broken dreams and shattered hopes.

jokes.text: That Simpson. He thinks he's the pope of chili town.
jokes.setup: Wiggum complaining about Homer
jokes.punchline: Ridiculous title 'pope of chili town'
jokes.explanation: Absurd religious metaphor applied to chili competition - memorable phrasing

dialogue.speaker: Chief Wiggum
dialogue.line: That Homer Simpson! He's always causing trouble. And now he's got some ridiculous title 'pope of chili town'.

jokes.text: The merciless peppers of Quetzlzacatenango!
jokes.setup: Wiggum revealing his secret chili ingredient
jokes.punchline: Impossibly complex Guatemalan place name
jokes.explanation: Fantastic made-up place name that sounds authentic - great escalation of the pepper mythology

dialogue.speaker: Chief Wiggum
dialogue.line: The secret ingredient is... love!

dialogue.speaker: Chief Wiggum
dialogue.line: Just kidding, it's a heap of jalapeños.

jokes.text: Grown deep in the jungle primeval... by the inmates of a Guatemalan insane asylum.
jokes.setup: Building mythology around the peppers
jokes.punchline: Increasingly absurd and dark origin story
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation - each detail makes the peppers more mythologically dangerous

jokes.text: Uh, Wiggie, my chili's gettin' cold.
jokes.setup: Wiggum building elaborate pepper mythology
jokes.punchline: Homer completely missing the point, worried about temperature
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer obliviousness - completely missing the warning about dangerous peppers

dialogue.speaker: Chief Wiggum
dialogue.line: You see, the pepper is a complex creature with its own mythology and culture...

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Wait, wait... is it hot in here, or is it just me?

jokes.text: [Garbled] Oh, my God! It's burning up! Beer! Beer! Give me some beer!
jokes.setup: Homer eats the insanely spicy peppers
jokes.punchline: Immediate regret and desperate need for beer
jokes.explanation: Predictable but satisfying payoff to the pepper setup

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Woo-hoo! I'm gonna eat all these peppers!

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Mmm... peppers... Oh, that's nice. Oh!

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Ow! Ow! My mouth is on fire! Water! I need water!

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Beer! Where's my beer?! Mmm... beer...

jokes.text: Oh, lousy Wiggum. Now the whole town thinks I'm a chili wuss.
jokes.setup: Homer failed the pepper challenge publicly
jokes.punchline: Homer's wounded pride over his 'chili wuss' reputation
jokes.explanation: Good character work - Homer's specific masculine insecurity about chili tolerance

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Why does everyone keep calling me a chili wuss? I'm not a wuss! I'm a man!

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: Homer, you cried like a baby when you ate that pepper.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: That was different! My mouth was on fire!

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: Yeah Dad, you were screaming and rolling around on the ground.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: I was not! I was... executing a strategic tactical maneuver!

jokes.text: You don't want to get wax in your mouth, do you? Maybe I do, son. Maybe I do.
jokes.setup: Bart warns Homer he's drinking candle wax
jokes.punchline: Homer's defiant acceptance of self-harm
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about Homer's self-destructive pride - perfectly delivered

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: Dad, that's candle wax!

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: I know.

jokes.text: 'Oh, this porridge is too hot. Oh, wah, wah, wah!'
jokes.setup: Crowd mocking Homer's earlier failure
jokes.punchline: Goldilocks reference mocking Homer as a baby
jokes.explanation: Effective crowd mockery using fairy tale reference

dialogue.speaker: Crowd
dialogue.line: He's too fat! He's too dumb! He's juuust right!

jokes.text: His ears if we're lucky!
jokes.setup: Dr. Hibbert noting steam should come from Homer's ears
jokes.punchline: Implication that worse bodily harm is possible
jokes.explanation: Perfect dark escalation - very economical but effective

dialogue.speaker: Dr. Hibbert
dialogue.line: Well, if steam is coming from his ears, that's actually a sign of a much worse problem.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: What could be worse than steam coming from my ears?

dialogue.speaker: Dr. Hibbert
dialogue.line: Well, if it were coming from somewhere else, I'd be very concerned.

jokes.text: Well, Chief, don't quit your day job. [Chuckles] Whatever that is.
jokes.setup: Homer triumphant after eating the peppers
jokes.punchline: Insult followed by admission he doesn't know Wiggum's job
jokes.explanation: Great misdirection - starts as standard insult, ends with Homer's obliviousness

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: I'm the king of the world!

dialogue.speaker: Chief Wiggum
dialogue.line: Homer, you're an idiot.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Yeah, well at least I know what my job is!

dialogue.speaker: Chief Wiggum
dialogue.line: You're the safety inspector at the nuclear plant.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Right, right. So what's YOUR job again?

jokes.text: What can I do-diddly, do-diddly, doodily, diddly, doodily... hoppity, hippity, hoppity, hippity, gooble, gobble, gooble, gooble, gabba, gabba, hey?
jokes.setup: Homer asks hallucinating Flanders for help
jokes.punchline: Flanders' speech dissolving into nonsense
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation from Flanders' signature speech pattern to complete gibberish - perfectly shows Homer's hallucination

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Flanders, I'm hallucinating. Can you help me?

dialogue.speaker: Ned Flanders
dialogue.line: Well, howdy-diddly-doodly, Homer! I'd be tickled pink to lend a helping hand-erino!

dialogue.speaker: Ned Flanders
dialogue.line: Just focus on my voice and the blibber-blabber will flooba-dooba in your noggin-a-rooney!

jokes.text: Goo-goo-ga-joob? Ha-ha! Ha-ha!
jokes.setup: Flanders continues speaking nonsense
jokes.punchline: Beatles reference followed by mechanical laughter
jokes.explanation: Good follow-up to the gibberish with cultural reference

dialogue.speaker: Ned Flanders
dialogue.line: Well, I guess you could say I'm a real... twist and shout kind of fella! Okily-dokily!

dialogue.speaker: Audience
dialogue.line: *mechanical laughter*

jokes.text: Let him go, Ralph. He knows what he's doing.
jokes.setup: Homer clearly in distress and hallucinating
jokes.punchline: Chief Wiggum's confident assessment that Homer's fine
jokes.explanation: Perfect Wiggum incompetence - completely misreading an obvious emergency

dialogue.speaker: Chief Wiggum
dialogue.line: He's fine.

jokes.text: Man, this is crazy. I hope I didn't brain my damage.
jokes.setup: Homer trying to figure out what's happening to him
jokes.punchline: Homer mangles the phrase 'damage my brain'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homerism - the malapropism actually makes the concern more poignant and funny

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: What's happening to me? I can't think straight. Did I damage my brain? No wait... did I... brain my damage?

jokes.text: Note to self: Stop doing anything.
jokes.setup: Homer experiencing strange hallucinations
jokes.punchline: Homer's logical conclusion is complete inaction
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd Homer logic - complete paralysis as problem-solving strategy

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: I'm seeing things... pink elephants, dancing donuts... Wait, what if I'm already dead? Or what if I'm dreaming? Or what if the real world is actually the dream and I'm awake in some cosmic void?

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: You know what? All this thinking is making my brain hurt. I don't know what's real anymore, so the safest thing to do is... nothing.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: That's right. No moving, no talking, no thinking. If I just sit here perfectly still, maybe reality will sort itself out.

jokes.text: Homer made a total jackass of himself.
jokes.setup: Marge looking for Homer at the cook-off
jokes.punchline: Helen's blunt assessment of Homer's behavior
jokes.explanation: Standard Helen gossip but effective for character

dialogue.speaker: Helen Lovejoy
dialogue.line: Well, if it isn't Homer Simpson. Always making a spectacle of himself at these community events. Think of the children!

jokes.text: But someone had to tell her. And I got here first.
jokes.setup: Rev. Lovejoy tells Helen to stop gossiping
jokes.punchline: Helen's competitive approach to spreading bad news
jokes.explanation: Good insight into Helen's gossipy nature - treating bad news like a race

dialogue.speaker: Rev. Lovejoy
dialogue.line: Helen, you need to stop gossiping about the parishioners.

dialogue.speaker: Helen Lovejoy
dialogue.line: But Reverend, if I don't tell people about their sins, how will they know to be ashamed?

jokes.text: I guess I know what a promise from Homer J. Simpson is worth.
jokes.setup: Learning Homer broke his promise not to drink
jokes.punchline: Marge's bitter assessment of Homer's reliability
jokes.explanation: Standard marital disappointment humor but well-delivered

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: Homer, you promised you wouldn't drink anymore.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: I know, but...

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: Your promises are like your New Year's resolutions — forgotten by January 2nd.

jokes.text: Hey, buddy! Get back there with the other rocks.
jokes.setup: Homer hallucinating, lying among rocks
jokes.punchline: Stranger mistakes Homer for an inanimate object
jokes.explanation: Good visual gag showing Homer's catatonic state

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Why is everything so... spinny?

dialogue.speaker: Stranger
dialogue.line: Hey, this rock looks comfortable. Mind if I sit on you? I mean, it?

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Mmm... rocks...

jokes.text: When I'm kicking you, that means hurry up.
jokes.setup: Homer following a tortoise
jokes.punchline: Homer explaining violence to an animal as communication
jokes.explanation: Homer's casual cruelty delivered matter-of-factly

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Hey, Mr. Tortoise! Want to see something cool?

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: This is how we communicate where I come from.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Woo-hoo!

jokes.text: This is 'cause I kicked you, isn't it?
jokes.setup: Tortoise leads Homer to difficult climb
jokes.punchline: Homer assumes the tortoise is being vindictive
jokes.explanation: Good callback showing Homer's paranoia and anthropomorphizing

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Why you little...

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: He's getting me back for that time I stepped on him!

jokes.text: You're just mad 'cause there's no clock in your hat.
jokes.setup: Lisa commenting on Bart's chili hat
jokes.punchline: Bart's nonsensical insult about Lisa's non-existent hat
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd Bart logic - making up problems to insult Lisa

dialogue.speaker: Lisa
dialogue.line: Bart, why are you wearing a chili hat?

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: Eat my shorts, Lisa! At least I don't wear a stupid... uh... whatever hat you're not wearing!

jokes.text: What hat? [Scoffs] Ah, this baby's wasted on an idiot like you.
jokes.setup: Bart's nonsensical insult about Lisa's hat
jokes.punchline: Lisa's logical response and Bart's dismissal of logic itself
jokes.explanation: Good sibling dynamic - Bart dismissing Lisa's intelligence when caught in nonsense

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: Your hat's so lame, it makes a dead guy look alive!

dialogue.speaker: Lisa
dialogue.line: Bart, that doesn't even make logical sense. A dead person can't look alive because they're, by definition, dead.

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: Oh, here we go with the logic thing. You know what? Logic is for nerds.

jokes.text: Oh, now what? If you're still mad at me, I'm gonna be really mad.
jokes.setup: Homer can't find Marge in hallucination
jokes.punchline: Homer threatens to be mad at his hallucination
jokes.explanation: Good Homer obliviousness - treating hallucination like real marital dispute

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Marge? Marge! Marge, where are you?

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: If you don't show up, I'm gonna be mad!

jokes.text: Come on, Marge. Will you- Fight fair! I never do this to you.
jokes.setup: Homer can't find Marge in his hallucination
jokes.punchline: Homer complains about fairness in his own mind
jokes.explanation: Excellent character work - Homer applying marital logic to hallucinations

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Hey! This isn't fair! I'm paying for this hallucination with my own brain!

jokes.text: The problem, Homer, is that the mind is always chattering away... with a thousand thoughts at once. Yeah. That's me all right.
jokes.setup: Coyote describing mental chatter
jokes.punchline: Homer agreeing he has too many thoughts
jokes.explanation: Perfect irony - Homer, famously empty-headed, agreeing he thinks too much

dialogue.speaker: Space Coyote
dialogue.line: You have too many voices in your head.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: I know. Shut up!

jokes.text: Are you kiddin'? [Chuckles] If anything, you should get more possessions. You don't even have a computer.
jokes.setup: Homer asks if he should get rid of possessions for enlightenment
jokes.punchline: Spirit guide advocates materialism instead
jokes.explanation: Brilliant subversion of spiritual journey tropes - the guide promotes consumerism

dialogue.speaker: Homer Simpson
dialogue.line: So if I get rid of all my possessions, I'll achieve enlightenment?

dialogue.speaker: Space Coyote
dialogue.line: No, man. You should totally keep your stuff. Possessions are awesome. You need a TV, a stereo, maybe some cool lamps. Enlightenment is overrated anyway.

jokes.text: Sorry. [Chuckles] I am a coyote.
jokes.setup: Coyote acting aggressively
jokes.punchline: Casual apology explaining behavior by species
jokes.explanation: Good character work - supernatural being with ordinary manners

dialogue.speaker: Space Coyote
dialogue.line: I'm sorry, man. It's just that we coyotes are very territorial.

jokes.text: Look. Just give me some inner peace, or I'll mop the floor with ya!
jokes.setup: Homer seeking spiritual enlightenment
jokes.punchline: Homer threatens violence to get inner peace
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer paradox - using aggression to demand tranquility

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: I'm seeking spiritual enlightenment.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: If I don't find inner peace, I'm gonna punch somebody!

jokes.text: Great spiritual quest, wolfie! My soul mate is Marge.
jokes.setup: Coyote telling Homer to find his soul mate
jokes.punchline: Homer's dismissive attitude and wrong animal identification
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Homer's impatience with mysticism and poor animal identification.

dialogue.speaker: Coyote
dialogue.line: You must find your soul mate, Homer.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Soul mate? I'm already married.

dialogue.speaker: Coyote
dialogue.line: No, your true soul mate. The one you are destined to be with.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Oh, you mean like a hot chick? Forget it, I'm not interested.

dialogue.speaker: Coyote
dialogue.line: It is your destiny!

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Look, I don't have time for this mystical coyote stuff. Besides, aren't you a dog?

jokes.text: A ghost train! And so little time to get out of the way. Now less. Now none!
jokes.setup: Homer seeing what he thinks is a ghost train
jokes.punchline: Homer's increasingly panicked countdown with no actual movement
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of panic with mathematical precision. The countdown structure works well.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Five! Four! Three! Two! One! Ahhhh!

jokes.text: Did I dream that whole thing? Maybe the desert was just this sand trap. Oh, and I bet that crazy pyramid was just the pro shop.
jokes.setup: Homer waking up on golf course
jokes.punchline: Homer rationalizing his mystical experience as mundane
jokes.explanation: Good character work - Homer's practical mind trying to explain the mystical

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Woo-hoo! I'm alive! Wait, where am I?

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: A golf course? How did I get here? Last thing I remember, I was... I was...

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: You know what? I don't need to understand everything. There's probably a perfectly logical explanation. I must've sleepwalked here after eating too much pepperoni pizza before bed.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Mmmm... pepperoni pizza...

jokes.text: And that talkin' coyote was really just a talkin' dog. Hi, Homer. Find your soul mate.
jokes.setup: Homer rationalizing the talking coyote
jokes.punchline: Real talking dog appears, undermining his rationalization
jokes.explanation: Brilliant misdirection - just as Homer rationalizes the mystical, reality becomes absurd

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: I know what I saw. That coyote was real and it talked to me.

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: Homer, you were eating peyote.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: So? That doesn't mean—

dialogue.speaker: Santa's Little Helper
dialogue.line: Homer, your wife is right. You were hallucinating.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: D'oh!

jokes.text: Hey. Wait a minute! There's no such thing as a talkin' dog. [Barks] Damn straight.
jokes.setup: Homer realizes dogs can't talk
jokes.punchline: Dog confirms this by talking
jokes.explanation: Perfect logical contradiction delivered with complete confidence - brilliant absurdist comedy

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Wait, dogs can't talk!

dialogue.speaker: Dog
dialogue.line: That's right, we can't.

jokes.text: Oh, honey. I didn't get drunk. I just went to a strange fantasy world.
jokes.setup: Marge explaining her anger about Homer's drinking
jokes.punchline: Homer's defense that doesn't actually address the drinking accusation
jokes.explanation: Good Homer evasion that's technically true but completely misses the point. Character-consistent logic.

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: Homer, I'm sick of your drinking! It's destroying this family!

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Marge, that's not fair. I only drink on days that end in 'y'.

jokes.text: Don't soul mate me!
jokes.setup: Homer calling Marge his soul mate after breaking promise
jokes.punchline: Marge turning 'soul mate' into an action verb to reject it
jokes.explanation: Creative wordplay showing Marge's anger through linguistic invention

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: Soul mate? Homer, you break every promise you make. I'm not your soul mate, I'm your soul servant.

jokes.text: Look at these records. Jim Nabors, Glen Campbell, the Doodletown Pipers. Now look at her records. They stink!
jokes.setup: Homer comparing his and Marge's music taste
jokes.punchline: Homer thinks his terrible music is superior
jokes.explanation: Good character comedy - Homer's complete lack of musical taste awareness

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Marge, I've been thinking. Your music taste is terrible.

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: Homer, that's not fair. What's wrong with my music?

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Well, it's boring and sophisticated. My music, on the other hand, is sophisticated AND boring.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: So clearly, I win.

jokes.text: This really goes beyond my training as a furniture salesman, sir.
jokes.setup: Homer asking furniture salesman about soul mates
jokes.punchline: Salesman's professional boundary acknowledgment
jokes.explanation: Good deadpan response to Homer's inappropriate question

dialogue.speaker: Homer Simpson
dialogue.line: So you're telling me you've never found your soul mate?

dialogue.speaker: Furniture Salesman
dialogue.line: Well, I'm a furniture salesman, not a therapist. That'll be $3,000.

jokes.text: Okay. But I'm not your soul mate. I'm really more of a chum.
jokes.setup: Homer suggesting Barney might be his soul mate
jokes.punchline: Barney's specific downgrade of their relationship
jokes.explanation: Good setup for the relationship hierarchy gag

dialogue.speaker: Barney
dialogue.line: Soul mate? Homer, I love you, but let's be honest — you're more of a drinking buddy. And even then, only when I'm desperate.

jokes.text: I'm a well-wisher... in that I don't wish you any specific harm.
jokes.setup: Moe defining his relationship with Homer
jokes.punchline: Damning with faint praise taken to logical extreme
jokes.explanation: Perfect Moe - brutal honesty disguised as politeness, perfectly capping the escalation

dialogue.speaker: Moe
dialogue.line: Look, Homer and me, we're not exactly friends. But I'll tell ya, if he ever needed a kidney, I'd give him one of mine.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Really?

dialogue.speaker: Moe
dialogue.line: Yeah... I got two. I can live with one.

jokes.text: This is just your memory. I can't give you any new information.
jokes.setup: Homer asking hallucination for help
jokes.punchline: Hallucination explains its limitations logically
jokes.explanation: Meta-humor about the nature of memory and hallucination

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Can you help me?

dialogue.speaker: Space Coyote
dialogue.line: I can only speak to you in visions and dreams. I cannot help you in the real world.

jokes.text: A machine? Earl's a machine? Oh, that's just perfect! Homer's desperate search for a soul mate... has yielded a lighthouse-keepin' robot!
jokes.setup: Homer expecting to find lonely human lighthouse keeper
jokes.punchline: Earl turns out to be an automated machine
jokes.explanation: Good payoff to the lighthouse setup - Homer's dramatic disappointment

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Hello? Is anybody here?

dialogue.speaker: Automated Voice
dialogue.line: Welcome to the automated lighthouse. Please state your business.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Oh no, it's a machine! Where's Earl?

dialogue.speaker: Automated Voice
dialogue.line: Earl was deactivated in 1987. I am his replacement.

jokes.text: Either that, or Batman's really let himself go.
jokes.setup: Seeing Homer's silhouette at lighthouse
jokes.punchline: Bart's pop culture reference comparing Homer to fat Batman
jokes.explanation: Good visual observation with pop culture twist

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: Whoa, check it out — it's like the Bat Signal, except it's just a really fat guy blocking out the light.

jokes.text: Alone! I'm alone! I'm a lonely, insignificant speck... on a has-been planet orbited by a cold, indifferent sun!
jokes.setup: Homer feeling isolated
jokes.punchline: Homer's cosmic-scale self-pity
jokes.explanation: Good escalation from personal to cosmic despair - surprisingly poetic for Homer

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Why does everything bad happen to me? I'm the most pathetic person in the universe. Even the stars are laughing at me. I bet aliens are up there right now, pointing at Earth going, 'Look at that loser down there. That's Homer Simpson.'

jokes.text: Uh, would that be the hot pants, sir? Aye. The hot pants.
jokes.setup: Ship carrying mysterious 'precious cargo'
jokes.punchline: The precious cargo is just hot pants
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist humor - treating clothing as precious cargo with naval seriousness

dialogue.speaker: Ship crew member
dialogue.line: Captain, we've safely delivered the precious cargo.

dialogue.speaker: Captain
dialogue.line: Excellent! What is this mysterious cargo?

dialogue.speaker: Ship crew member
dialogue.line: Hot pants, sir.

jokes.text: And then I saw the lighthouse... and I remembered how you love blinking lights... like the one on the waffle iron. Or that little guy on the Don't Walk sign.
jokes.setup: Marge explaining her deductive process
jokes.punchline: Marge's specific knowledge of Homer's attraction to simple blinking lights
jokes.explanation: Excellent character detail showing the depth of Marge's understanding of Homer's simple pleasures.

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: Well, I deduced it from the fact that Homer is always staring at the TV, and the only thing that keeps his attention is when there are blinking lights on the screen.

jokes.text: See, I thought we weren't soul mates because- We had a fight? Right. And we don't like the same things. It's like you're from Venus- And you're from Mars.
jokes.setup: Homer explaining why he thought they weren't soul mates
jokes.punchline: Marge immediately understands and completes his thoughts
jokes.explanation: Good character work showing their actual compatibility through understanding

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: I thought we weren't soul mates because you were smart and I was dumb.

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: And you thought that meant we couldn't understand each other.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Exactly!

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: But we do understand each other, Homer. That's what makes us soul mates.

jokes.text: Oh, sure. Give me the one with all the monsters.
jokes.setup: Marge says Homer is from Mars
jokes.punchline: Homer's immediate complaint about Mars being scary
jokes.explanation: Good Homer logic - immediate victimhood about planet assignment

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Mars? Scary? What if there's Martians? What if they're mean?

jokes.text: Our differences are only skin deep. But our 'sames' go right down to the bone.
jokes.setup: Marge explaining their relationship
jokes.punchline: Marge's made-up word 'sames' in heartfelt moment
jokes.explanation: Sweet character moment with gentle wordplay - Marge creating new word for emotional truth

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: Homer and I have our sames and our differents.

jokes.text: In your face, space coyote!
jokes.setup: Homer and Marge reconciling as soul mates
jokes.punchline: Homer's triumphant taunt to his hallucinated spirit guide
jokes.explanation: Good callback showing Homer's childish victory celebration directed at his own hallucination.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Take that, spiritual guide!

jokes.text: Jonathan Livingston Seagull! We're on a collision course!
jokes.setup: Ship heading toward rocks
jokes.punchline: Captain's literary reference as an expletive
jokes.explanation: Creative use of literary reference as maritime curse

dialogue.speaker: Ship Captain
dialogue.line: Full steam ahead! We're heading straight for those rocks!

tmdb_overview: After eating a dish laced with potent peppers during the chili cookoff, Homer has hallucinations where a mystical coyote urges him to find his soulmate.
insight: Psychedelic trip episode sustains absurdist comedy across 77 jokes despite uneven craft.
